name: Check `n Go
description_short: Check `n Go is a national payday and installment loan provider. The Detroit East Jefferson location offers same-day cash advances with a 5.0-star rating from over 1,000 customers.
description_long:
Check `n Go is a national consumer lending company with a physical branch at 13338 East Jefferson Avenue in Detroit, Michigan. The company operates across multiple states and makes loans available through three channels: in-store, online, and by phone where available. The Detroit location has accumulated over 1,021 Google reviews with a perfect 5.0-star average, with reviewers frequently naming individual staff members — Tammy, Kiara — by name and citing fast, professional, and stress-free service.

The East Jefferson branch offers payday loans — short-term advances repaid by the borrower's next payday — as well as installment loans for customers who need a longer repayment structure. In addition to loan products, the branch sells the Netspend Prepaid Mastercard, a reloadable card usable for bill payment and shopping without requiring a traditional bank account. Funding is same-day for in-store applicants and typically the next business day for online applicants. No Cost Extended Payment Plans are available for borrowers who need additional flexibility on repayment.

What sets this location apart from typical payday lenders is the volume and consistency of its customer reviews: over 1,000 ratings at a 5.0 average is uncommon in a high-volume short-term lending environment. Application requirements are clearly stated — valid government-issued ID, proof of income, a checking account active for at least 30 days, and a working phone number — which reduces surprises during the process. The ability to begin an application in-store and upload documentation later adds a degree of convenience.

Honestly, this is still a payday lending operation. Short-term payday loans carry fees that translate to very high annualized rates — a structural reality the website does not address directly. The requirement for a 30-day-active checking account means the most financially excluded customers (fully unbanked) may not qualify for loans even if they can obtain the Netspend card. The company's website was also down for maintenance at the time of research, which raises minor reliability concerns for customers attempting to apply or manage loans online. Best suited to borrowers who need fast, in-person service and have exhausted lower-cost options.
pros: Same-day funding available for in-store applicants
5.0-star Google rating from 1,021+ reviews — unusually strong for a payday lender
No Cost Extended Payment Plans available for repayment flexibility
Multiple application channels: in-store, online, and phone
Netspend Prepaid Mastercard available for customers without traditional bank accounts
Clear, upfront application requirements listed — no hidden documentation surprises
Bad or no credit applicants encouraged to apply; credit is a factor but not an automatic disqualifier
cons: Payday loan fees translate to very high APR — the website does not disclose rates
Requires checking account active for at least 30 days, excluding some unbanked customers
Limited hours: closes 6PM weekdays, 2PM Saturday, closed Sunday
Website was down for maintenance at time of research — may affect online applicants
Loan amounts are limited by payday loan structure; not suitable for larger financial needs
best_for: Borrowers who need cash before their next payday and prefer face-to-face service
People with bad or no credit who have been declined elsewhere
Detroit-area residents who want same-day in-store funding
Customers seeking a prepaid Mastercard alongside a short-term loan
diagnosis: Check `n Go Detroit is best for borrowers who need fast, in-person emergency cash and have limited credit history, and who are within reach of the East Jefferson Avenue branch during its limited hours. The main caveat is cost: payday loans are expensive short-term instruments and no APR or fee information is published on the location page, so borrowers should ask directly before signing.
